instructions.when: Once per database solution. This file sits at the root of the database's solution folder and defines the engine and hosting. Each table/model is a separate file in the same folder (copies of _forms/database-model.json).
instructions.how: Answer in plain English. Then hand it to Claude using the bridge in claude-only/1-translate-to-claude.md.
instructions.note: Solution = the folder this file sits in.

databaseType.question: Which database type does it use?
databaseType.guidance: Be specific — this defines the database engine for every model in this solution.
databaseType.example: PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, SQL Server, MongoDB, DynamoDB, Redis.

hosting.question: Where will it be deployed / hosted?
hosting.example: Firebase, Azure SQL, Azure Elastic Pool, Azure Cosmos DB.

cannotDo.question: What can this solution NOT do / not deploy?
cannotDo.guidance: Constraints the AI must respect when building against this database.
cannotDo.example: No SQL Agent Jobs on an Elastic Pool.

scaffold.guidance: How this solution's repo is laid out and driven, so the AI can create the skeleton on the first build and verify its work on every build. Pick a boilerplate from _forms/boilerplates.json by name, or fill the fields in yourself — anything you fill in overrides the boilerplate's version of it.

fileLayout.question: What is the folder and file layout of this solution's repo?
fileLayout.guidance: One entry per folder or key file, with what belongs in it.

testing.question: What test framework should be used, and what kinds of tests?
testing.example: tSQLt; uniqueness, nullability, and relationship checks per database-model brief.

commands.question: The commands the AI should use to work with this repo.
